text.title: ops-annual-planning-process Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ops-annual-planning-process as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Plan has 6 annual priorities and no "not doing this year" list.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Six priorities means nothing is prioritized. Without a stop-doing list, the founder keeps all existing commitments plus adds new ones. Compounding never happens.

failure-mode.description: Quarterly plan decomposes into daily task lists generated in January.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Daily tasks from January are stale by March. Cascade only to monthly level; weekly and daily plans emerge from current context, not annual planning.

failure-mode.description: Year-in-review contains only wins. Churned customers and missed targets are not mentioned.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Survivorship bias in annual reviews produces optimistic plans that ignore the mechanisms of past failure. Year-in-review must include lost-deal and churned-customer themes.

failure-mode.description: Annual plan has financial and product goals but no personal goals (energy, satisfaction, burnout).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Founder burnout kills the plan. A solopreneur's sustainability is a business risk, not a soft concern.
